# WriteKit — MCP-powered publishing tool

> WriteKit lets you publish and manage pages and collections entirely through conversation with an AI assistant using the Model Context Protocol. No editor, no dashboard.

## Quick start

```
claude mcp add --transport http writekit https://writekit.dev/mcp
```

Authentication happens automatically on first tool use — a browser window opens to sign in via OAuth (GitHub, Google, Discord) or magic link.

## MCP Server URL

https://writekit.dev/mcp

## Tools

### create_page
Create a new page (starts as draft).
- Required: title (string), content (string, rich markdown)
- Optional: excerpt (string), tags (string[]), slug (string), collection_id (string), position (int), visibility (public|unlisted|private, default public), tenant_id (string)
- Returns: page ID, slug, preview URL
- Content supports: GFM, tables, footnotes, syntax-highlighted code blocks, callout blocks, media embeds, D2 diagrams, images, raw HTML

### update_page
Update an existing page. Partial updates — only send fields to change.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: title, content, excerpt, tags, slug, collection_id, position, visibility (public|unlisted|private), tenant_id
- Returns: updated page info, preview URL

### delete_page
Permanently delete a page.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### publish_page
Publish a draft page. Sets status=published, published_at=now.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: tenant_id
- Returns: live URL

### unpublish_page
Revert a published page to draft.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### list_pages
List pages with optional filters.
- Optional: status (draft|published), visibility (public|unlisted|private), tag (string), collection_id (string, use "standalone" for uncollected pages), limit (int), tenant_id
- Returns: titles, slugs, statuses, tags, collection info, published dates

### get_page
Get a single page with full markdown content.
- Required: id (string) OR slug (string)
- Optional: tenant_id
- Returns: full page including raw markdown content

### append_to_page
Append markdown content to the end of an existing page. Use instead of update_page when adding new sections.
- Required: id (string), content (string, markdown to append)
- Optional: tenant_id

### search_pages
Full-text search across page titles and content (FTS5).
- Required: query (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### create_collection
Create a new collection to group pages.
- Required: title (string)
- Optional: description (string), slug (string), sort_order ("manual"|"date", default "manual"), visibility (public|unlisted|private, default public), tenant_id

### update_collection
Update a collection. Partial — only send fields to change.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: title, description, slug, sort_order, visibility (public|unlisted|private), tenant_id

### delete_collection
Delete a collection. Pages in the collection become standalone.
- Required: id (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### list_collections
List all collections with page counts.
- Optional: tenant_id

### get_collection
Get collection details and its pages.
- Required: id (string) OR slug (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### reorder_pages
Set the order of pages within a manually-ordered collection.
- Required: collection_id (string), page_ids (string[])
- Optional: tenant_id

### get_settings
Get all site settings (title, description, code_theme).
- Optional: tenant_id

### update_settings
Update site settings. Partial — only send keys to change.
- Optional: title (string), description (string), code_theme (string), tenant_id

## Teams

### list_members
List all team members for a site.
- Optional: tenant_id

### invite_member
Invite someone to the site team by email. Sends an email with an accept link. If the invitee has no WriteKit account, they create one when they accept. Invitations expire after 14 days. Requires owner role.
- Required: email (string), role (editor|viewer)
- Optional: tenant_id

### list_invitations
List pending invitations (not yet accepted or revoked).
- Optional: tenant_id

### resend_invitation
Generate a new link for a pending invitation and extend expiry. Requires owner role.
- Required: email (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### revoke_invitation
Cancel a pending invitation. The link stops working. Requires owner role.
- Required: email (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### remove_member
Remove a team member. Cannot remove the last owner. Requires owner role.
- Required: email (string)
- Optional: tenant_id

### update_member_role
Change a team member's role. Cannot demote the last owner. Requires owner role.
- Required: email (string), role (owner|editor|viewer)
- Optional: tenant_id

## Prompts

### write_page
Generate a well-structured page about a topic.
- Required: topic (string) — What to write about
- Optional: audience (string) — Target audience (e.g., beginners, senior devs)
- Optional: style (string) — Writing style (e.g., tutorial, opinion, guide)

## Resources

- writekit://site/stats — page counts, collection counts
- writekit://site/settings — current site settings as JSON
- writekit://site/recent-pages — last 10 published pages
- writekit://site/drafts — all draft pages
- writekit://site/collections — all collections with page counts

## Tenant resolution

If a user has one site, it's selected automatically. If multiple sites exist, pass tenant_id on each tool call.

## Roles

Sites have team members with roles:
- **owner**: Full control — billing, settings, team management, content
- **editor**: Create, edit, publish pages and collections. Can view private content.
- **viewer**: Can view private content only.

Write operations (create/update/delete/publish) require editor role. Team management and site settings require owner role.

## Visibility

Pages and collections have a visibility setting:
- **public** (default): Shown everywhere — index, sitemap, search
- **unlisted**: Accessible via direct URL but hidden from index, sitemap, and search
- **private**: Only visible to authenticated team members. Returns 404 for everyone else.

A private collection hides all its pages regardless of their individual visibility.

## Content format

Pages use Markdown rendered with Goldmark and Chroma syntax highlighting. Supported features:

### Basic Markdown
- Headings (# through ######) with auto-generated IDs
- **Bold**, *italic*, ~~strikethrough~~
- Links, images (by URL)
- Ordered/unordered lists, task lists
- Tables (GFM), horizontal rules, footnotes ([^1])
- Raw HTML

### Syntax-highlighted code blocks
Use fenced code blocks with language tags for rich rendering with language icons and copy buttons:
```go
func main() {
    fmt.Println("Hello, world!")
}
```
Supported languages include Go, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Rust, Java, Ruby, and 50+ more.

### Callout blocks
Start a blockquote with a type marker for styled alert boxes:
```
> [!NOTE] This renders as a blue info box with an icon
> [!TIP] Green tip box — great for best practices
> [!WARNING] Yellow warning box — watch out!
> [!DANGER] Red danger box — critical issues
```
Also supports: [!INFO], [!IMPORTANT], [!CAUTION]

### Media embeds
Use `<embed src="url" />` for rich media:
- YouTube — renders thumbnail with play button, loads iframe on click
- Spotify — track, album, playlist, episode, or show embeds
- SoundCloud — player embed
- Twitter/X — tweet embed with widgets.js
- GitHub Gists — loaded dynamically

### D2 diagrams
Use `d2` fenced code blocks for architecture and flow diagrams. Rendered server-side to SVG with NeutralGrey theme, dagre auto-layout, and interactive zoom/pan/fullscreen. D2 compilation errors are surfaced as warnings so the AI can fix them.

Key syntax:
- Containers: `server: { api: API; db: Database }` — group related components
- Shapes: `shape: cylinder` (DB), `queue`, `cloud`, `package`, `diamond`, `person`, `sql_table`, `class`
- Connections: `a -> b: label`, `a <-> b` (bidirectional), `a -- b` (undirected)
- Classes: `classes: { svc: { style.fill: "#3498DB" } }` then `node.class: svc`
- Styles: `style.fill`, `style.stroke`, `style.stroke-width`, `style.border-radius`, `style.shadow`, `style.3d`, `style.opacity`
- Icons: `icon: https://icons.terrastruct.com/...`
- Grid layout: `grid-rows: N`, `grid-columns: N` for structured layouts
- Sequence diagrams: `shape: sequence_diagram`
- Direction: `direction: right` (or down, left, up)

Example:
```d2
classes: {
  svc: { style.fill: "#3498DB"; style.stroke: "#2E5C8A"; style.font-color: "#fff" }
  store: { shape: cylinder; style.fill: "#2ECC71"; style.stroke: "#27AE60"; style.font-color: "#fff" }
}

frontend: Web App { class: svc }
backend: {
  api: API Gateway { class: svc }
  auth: Auth Service { class: svc }
  api -> auth: validate token
}
db: PostgreSQL { class: store }
cache: Redis { class: store; shape: queue }

frontend -> backend.api: REST/JSON
backend.api -> db: SQL
backend.api -> cache: session data
```

## Site URLs

- Site index: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev
- Standalone page: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/{slug}
- Collection index: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/{collection-slug}
- Page in collection: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/{collection-slug}/{page-slug}
- Raw markdown: append `.md` to any page URL (e.g. `/{slug}.md` or `/{collection}/{page}.md`)
- Search: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/search?q={query}
- Preview: https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/preview/{token} (expiring)

## Example prompts

- "Write a getting-started guide and add it to the docs collection"
- "Create a collection called 'Tutorials' with manual ordering"
- "Publish my getting-started page and move it to position 1"
- "Show me all my draft pages"
- "Update my auth page — add a warning callout about rate limits"
- "List all my collections"
- "Change my site title to 'Acme Docs'"
